data.name: The Correspondent: A Novel
data.description: An intimate novel about the transformative power of the written word and the beauty of slowing down to reconnect with the people we love. Told through letters, it follows Sybil Van Antwerp, a woman in her seventies, as she confronts her past and seeks forgiveness.

specs.value: Maggi-Meg Reed, Jane Oppenheimer, Carly Robins, Jeff Ebner, David Pittu, Chris Andrew Ciulla, Mark Bramhall, Petrea Burchard, Robert Petkoff, Kimberly Farr, Cerris Morgan-Moyer, Peter Ganim, Jade Wheeler, Various

response.name: The Correspondent: A Novel
response.description: An intimate novel about the transformative power of the written word and the beauty of slowing down to reconnect with the people we love. Told through letters, it follows Sybil Van Antwerp, a woman in her seventies, as she confronts her past and seeks forgiveness.

specs.value: Maggi-Meg Reed, Jane Oppenheimer, Carly Robins, Jeff Ebner, David Pittu, Chris Andrew Ciulla, Mark Bramhall, Petrea Burchard, Robert Petkoff, Kimberly Farr, Cerris Morgan-Moyer, Peter Ganim, Jade Wheeler, Various

response.name: The Correspondent: A Novel
response.description: A novel about the transformative power of the written word and the beauty of slowing down to reconnect with the people we love. The story is told through letters and emails, focusing on the life of Sybil Van Antwerp, a woman who uses letters to make sense of the world and her place in it.

specs.value: Maggi-Meg Reed, Jane Oppenheimer, Carly Robins, Jeff Ebner, David Pittu, Chris Andrew Ciulla, Mark Bramhall, Petrea Burchard, Robert Petkoff, Kimberly Farr, Cerris Morgan-Moyer, Peter Ganim, Jade Wheeler, Various
#6 in Audible Books & Originals, #1 in Family Life Fiction (Books), #1 in Epistolary Fiction (Audible Books & Originals), #1 in Women's Domestic Life Fiction
